# Suffixes

A suffix is a DNS-like string that is associated with an org or project scope.
Boundary appends the suffix to any aliases that are created in the scope.

You must configure a suffix to create an alias in the org or project scopes.
Creating aliases at the org and project scope level allows scope administrators to manage their own aliases, seeing only the resources that belong to their scope.
Organizations can also reuse alias values across org and project scopes where it makes sense.

## Attributes

Suffixes have the following attributes:

- `-scope-id=<string>` - Scope in which to create the suffix.
  You can also specify the scope using the BOUNDARY_SCOPE_ID environment variable.
  Suffixes are only supported for org and project scopes.
- `-alias-suffix=<string>` - Specifies the string that you want to use as the suffix. Boundary appends the suffix to any aliases you create in the scope you specified.
  The `alias-suffix` must comply with DNS naming rules.


## More information

- [Aliases](/boundary/docs/concepts/aliases)
